title: quercus peduncularis subsp. eupeduncularis
intro: Quercus peduncularis subsp. eupeduncularis is a subspecies of the Quercus peduncularis, a type of oak tree native to specific regions of China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Quercus peduncularis subsp. eupeduncularis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support cardiovascular and respiratory health.
